question: Across three ONNX quantization dtypes (fp32, fp16, q8) for the local query-side embedder (transformers.js, nomic-embed-text-v1.5), which dtype's query vectors retrieve the same top-10 results from the SYNTHETIC-embedded message_vectors matrix as a SYNTHETIC-embedded query vector -- and at what per-query latency / resident RAM cost?

priorRun.summary: q8 baseline: mean overlap@10 0.77 (gate 0.8 FAIL), top1 0.725 (pass); hypothesis was q8 quantization noise

methodology.queries: SAME 40 queries reused verbatim from the prior run (20 session/doc themes, 10 short, 10 long) -- scratchpad/parity/queries.json, byte-identical
methodology.syntheticVectors: REUSED from the prior run (scratchpad/parity/synthetic-vectors.json), not re-embedded -- same synthetic API query vectors for the same query text, avoiding a redundant API call
methodology.localEmbed: host process, packages/server/src/localOnnxEmbeddings.ts (makeLocalOnnxEmbedder), transformers.js pipeline dtype option set to 'fp32' / 'fp16' / 'q8' respectively, queryPrefix 'search_query: ', one fresh bun process per dtype (isolates resident RSS measurement per pipeline)
methodology.fp16Caveat: onnxruntime-node's default graph optimizations ('all') FAIL to initialize the fp16 checkpoint on this stack: LayerNormFusion references a node name removed by the fp16 precision-free-cast pass ('InsertedPrecisionFreeCast_/emb_ln/Constant_output_0'), error 'itr != node_args.end() was false'. Diagnosed via scratchpad/dtype/diag-fp16.ts. Workaround applied (fp16 ONLY): session_options.graphOptimizationLevel = 'disabled', via localOnnxEmbeddings.ts's existing pipelineFactory extension point (no production source changed). fp32 and q8 run with default 'all' optimizations, unmodified. CONSEQUENCE: fp16 latency numbers below are NOT apples-to-apples with fp32/q8 -- they run an unfused/unoptimized graph and are a pessimistic floor, not fp16's true achievable latency on a stack without this ORT bug.
methodology.embedLatency: warm pipeline (post model-load), 10 samples of a single-query embedMany([...]) call each, p50/p90/min/max reported in ms; pipelineLoadMs is the one-time warm-up call (includes model download on first run for that dtype/cache dir)
methodology.modelFileSize: size on disk (bytes) of the dtype-specific onnx file downloaded to a shared scratch cache dir, confirmed against HF hub Content-Length/X-Linked-Size
methodology.residentRss: process.memoryUsage() captured after pipeline warm-load, after the 10 single-query samples, and after the 40-query batch embed, in a dedicated bun process per dtype
methodology.matrixScan: docker exec inside quasar-server-server-1 (live sqlite in a docker-managed named volume, not host-reachable). Opened readonly (bun:sqlite {readonly:true}) with retry-on-open. All 4 vector sets (local-fp32, local-fp16, local-q8, synthetic) scored against ONE matrix load in the same process, so every dtype is compared against the identical live-matrix snapshot. No writes; no server process touched or restarted.
methodology.scoring: reuses vectorKernel.ts's encodeQueryVectorF16 + vectorBlob.ts's float16BitsToFloat32 decode, exact brute-force cosine over all rows for the model; spot-checked against cosineSimilarityF16Reference (maxDiff 0) before the full run.

matrix.source: live server sqlite (/data/quasar/quasar.sqlite), message_vectors table, read-only connection, in-container scan
matrix.note: row count differs slightly from the prior run (691271 -> 691290) -- live server continues ingesting between runs

perQuery.text: embedding cache dedup by content hash
simsimd f16 cosine kernel native dlopen
external staging disk support for ops
antigravity ingest data is trash delete
convex self hosted node action executor
Why does the readiness gate return a 503 error when the index diverges from the source of truth?
What caused the LanceDB disk usage to balloon after repeated optimize calls on the index?
How does the embedding cache deduplicate vectors by content hash to avoid re-embedding?
Why did the local ONNX embeddings fail to match the synthetic provider on the doc-level parity test?
What is the root cause of the twenty two second latency spike in semantic search queries?
How do we materialize missing vectors into SQLite for the resident matrix loader?
What is the difference between the query prefix and the document prefix for nomic embeddings?
How does the vector matrix worker pool shard cosine similarity scans across CPU cores?
Why do fresh sqlite connections against a live WAL database intermittently fail to open?
What is the proof that the docker staging materialization receipt can be trusted?
